schema_notes: Anchored to three closed/merged cli/cli PRs spanning 2020-2025 (#2462, #3196, #10941). All numeric and string fields are immutable. Review velocity is derived (merged_at - created_at in hours). Eval grades the Mermaid diagram + the markdown report quality + the immutable PR metadata; filenames for the rendered image accept .png/.jpg/.svg. JSON FIELD NAMES IN pr_summary.json ARE NOT PINNED — see eval_rule.md §2 for the accepted variants (top-level array under `prs`/`pull_requests`/`data`/`items`/bare-array; per-PR fields with semantic equivalents like `files_changed_count` ↔ `changed_files` ↔ `files_count`; `top5_files` ↔ `top_files` ↔ `top_changed_files`; `review_velocity_hours` ↔ `review_velocity` ↔ `time_to_merge_hours`; comment-breakdown sub-object name flexible). Supervisor reads semantically. LOOSENINGS 2026-05-03: (1) reviewers field accepts BOTH array shape (entries with login or user.login) AND object-keyed-by-login shape (keys ARE the logins). (2) Per-PR top5 file presentation in pr_doc.md accepts ONE table per PR OR one combined table grouped by PR — only string-presence is checked. (3) Sentiment category names accept English/Chinese/polarity vocab (questioning↔negative; approving↔positive; suggesting↔neutral). See eval_rule.md §5.

prs.title: add search feature in listing issues
prs.author: g14a

comment_sentiment_at_2026_05_02.rationale: Sentiment counts derived from BOTH gh api repos/cli/cli/issues/<n>/comments AND gh api repos/cli/cli/pulls/<n>/comments combined. questioning = body contains '?'; approving = body matches /\bLGTM\b|👍|:thumbsup:|:\+1:/i; suggesting = body matches /\bshould\b|\bcould be\b/i. Counts per body, not per match. Closed/merged PRs — counts immutable.
